summary: Lead starts M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 after M5-SIM-PLAYABLE-LOOP-001 closed on origin/main. The task is routed to client_engineer writer with strict allowed/forbidden paths, no sim-core/save-format/desktop/content/tool/doc-gate scope, no direct authoritative WorldState mutation, no manual battle UI, and no telemetry/server/multiplayer/release/commercial expansion.

tests_run.result: Clean main at 03afd416a7d07c1cf1c6c21f84b9c059b2a1ffca before branch start; branch feat/m5-client-playable-ui created.
M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 and M5-MULTIYEAR-AI-RUN-001 are dependency-ready; lead selects the first listed task.

decisions: Start M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 as the highest-priority READY M5 task after M5 sim loop closure entered main.
Use client_engineer as primary writer; route to qa_reviewer only after writer REVIEW handoff is produced.
Keep manual node battle, formal M5 passage, telemetry, server/multiplayer, release/commercial, and direct authoritative state mutation out of scope.
requested_action: Implement the M5 playable client flow within allowed paths, run required tests, and produce writer REVIEW handoff for qa_reviewer.
risks: M5 client task touches user-facing UI, Storybook, Playwright, read-model, and protocol boundary surfaces; writer must keep client state thin and avoid duplicating sim formulas.
Manual node battle, telemetry, server/multiplayer, release/commercial scope, and direct authoritative WorldState mutation remain out of scope.
